Hi Diana, thank you for your message. When would you like the report?
Good morning, Rafael. I would like it by tomorrow morning, please.
No problem. I am finishing the draft now. Could I add two new ideas?
Yes, please. Would you also attach the price list?
Sure. I will reply with everything in one email. Anything else?
That is perfect. Could you forward it to my assistant too? Thank you so much.
Could you send me the report?
I would like to confirm the meeting.
Please find the file attached.
Would you forward this to your team?
Thank you for your quick reply.

Hi Rafael, this is Diana from the conference last week. I am calling about a small request. Could you send me the sales report by email today? I would really like to read it before our meeting tomorrow. Please attach the file and add a clear subject line, so it is easy to find. If you are very busy this afternoon, would you forward it to my assistant instead? Her email address is in my last message. Thank you so much for your help. I am looking forward to your reply. Talk to you soon.

Hi Diana, this is Rafael. Thank you for your message this morning. I am writing the sales report right now, and I would like to send it this afternoon. I am finishing the last draft, and then I will reply to your email with the file. Could you check your inbox around three o'clock? I am also adding two new ideas for the project. If you have any questions, please call me back. I would be happy to explain everything. Thanks again, and have a great day. Talk to you soon.

Rafael opens his inbox early in the morning. He reads a polite request from a client. He writes a clear reply with a short subject line. He attaches the sales report to the email. Finally, he forwards a copy to his manager.
